meaning: n.操纵帆桁下部(使其上下的)索具
examples: He walked on to the stage to be greeted with jeers and whistles.
The speech of this fantastic figure was received with an explosion of jeers and laughter.
The minister's speech was greeted with jeers and catcalls.
Remember when he used to advise his little brother Andrew with Republican cheers or Democrat jeers?
And according to radio hosts like Scott Shannon, calls have gone from jeers to cheers.
